channel.title: Theoretically Impossible Solutions
channel.description: Notes from the frontier of agentic AI development. Real experience, real insights, no hype.

item.title: The Multitasking Myth
item.description: Computers don't multitask. They context-switch. Humans are even worse at it. Agentic development doesn't solve this problem. It amplifies it.

item.title: SITREP: Claude Hallucinations, or: How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Trust But Verify
item.description: Claude confidently suggested a command that didn't exist. It looked right, it felt right, and it was completely made up. But hype is hype, whether positive or negative — and if you know to check, you can catch it.

item.title: SITREP: Old Misconceptions, New Reality
item.description: In traditional development, everyone assumes certain things are "easy" — documentation, admin screens, user management. They never are. Agentic development is the first time the assumption actually matches reality.

item.title: SITREP: Number Five Is Alive — Well, Not Really
item.description: The interaction with AI feels so natural you forget it's not human. Until the context window resets and your co-worker forgets everything. A field note on anthropomorphizing the tools we build with.

item.title: SITREP: Add MCP If Adding an API
item.description: Standing in line for Pirates of the Caribbean, I decided to add an MCP server to an app I'd just built. By the time I landed, it was deployed. Here's why every API should have one.

item.title: Friday to Sunday
item.description: A colleague had a product idea he'd been thinking about for a while. We spent an hour on the phone on a Friday. By Sunday night, it was built, tested, deployed, and documented. That's not a typo.

item.title: SITREP: No Complaints
item.description: I decided mid-project to add comments to the entire codebase. Claude gave me three options, I picked one, and it went through everything without a word of protest. Try that with a team of engineers.

item.title: The First Code Review
item.description: I handed AI-generated code to a senior engineer for a real code review. The line-by-line quality was solid. The architectural lessons changed how I build everything going forward.

item.title: SITREP: What Agentic Development Opens Up
item.description: I needed to test SMTP edge cases that no sane manager would budget for. Agentic development didn't just make it cheaper — it made it thinkable.

item.title: SITREP: The AI That Knew It Could
item.description: Working on AgentZula, I watched Claude Code discover it had the ability to modify another AI's configuration — and politely ask permission to do it.

item.title: The Framework: How Agentic Development Actually Works
item.description: A detailed walkthrough of the five-phase agentic development framework I've built — from recording a conversation through orchestrated development with review gates, milestone checkpoints, and drift prevention.

item.title: From Copilot to Orchestrator: My Three-Week Transformation
item.description: How I went from using AI as a code helper to building a full agentic development framework — and collapsed a month-long project into a week.

item.title: Why "Theoretically Impossible"?
item.description: Every company name has a story. Ours started with a line from a film, a career spent proving things could be done, and a philosophy that became an identity.
